text:
Once upon a time, in a small wooden town nestled among hills draped in misty green, there lived a girl named Ada. Ada had eyes as deep as twilight forests and hair as blond as autumn leaves. She loved exploring caves in the mountains at dusk, discovering hidden treasures, and imagining stories about the creatures that lived within them.

One day, while wandering through an old growth forest, Ada stumbled upon an ancient oak tree. Something about the tree seemed peculiar; its bark wasn’t just bark. It pulsed with soft light like a moonlit river. When Ada touched it, she felt a warmth that seemed like a deep friendship.

Curiosity getting the better of her, Ada decided to explore deeper into the tree. As she did, she discovered a little clearing bathed in gentle bioluminescence. In the center stood an odd-looking bird, its feathers strangely human-like. Its head turned exactly once before speaking.

“Greetings, traveler from the land of the lightless woods. I
